data.title: Gold Resource (GORO) Q4 Profit Swing Challenges Bearish Loss-Making Narrative
data.description:
Advertisement

Gold Resource (GORO) FY 2025 earnings snapshot

Gold Resource (GORO) closed out FY 2025 with Q4 total revenue of US$51.3 million and basic EPS of US$0.11. On a trailing 12 month basis the company remained loss making with basic EPS at a US$0.05 loss on net income of a US$6.5 million loss. Over recent periods, total revenue has ranged from US$11.2 million to US$51.3 million a quarter, while quarterly basic EPS has swung between US$0.11 and a US$0.09 loss. This gives investors a clear look at the trade off between top line momentum and ongoing earnings pressure. The mix of stronger Q4 revenue and lingering TTM losses sets up a margin focused read through for anyone weighing the durability of this earnings turn.

See our full analysis for Gold Resource.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to set them against the most widely shared narratives around Gold Resource to see which stories still hold up and which need a rethink.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Q4 swings to US$18 million profit on prior losses

  • Q4 net income of US$18.0 million contrasts with a trailing 12 month net loss of US$6.5 million on US$99.8 million of revenue. The latest quarter therefore looks very different from the prior year of loss making results.
  • Bears argue that a company with a trailing 12 month loss and a five year pattern of widening losses at about 60.1% per year is still in a high risk zone, and the figures here partly back that up while also adding some tension:
    • Across FY 2025, three out of four quarters showed net losses between US$4.7 million and US$11.5 million, which aligns with the cautious view that profitability has not been consistent.
    • The sharp move from a US$14.3 million loss in Q4 FY 2024 to an US$18.0 million profit in Q4 FY 2025 gives bears fresh data to test whether this is a one off or the start of a more stable pattern.

Revenue nearly doubles quarter on quarter to US$51.3 million

  • Total revenue rose from US$24.9 million in Q3 FY 2025 to US$51.3 million in Q4. This also compares with US$12.0 million to US$13.3 million per quarter in late FY 2024, so the top line has moved around within a wide US$11.2 million to US$51.3 million range over the recent periods provided.
  • Optimistic investors often focus on revenue potential, and the forecast for revenue to grow about 12.2% per year supports that view, but the detailed numbers here give a more mixed picture:
    • Trailing 12 month revenue of US$99.8 million is higher than the US$65.7 million level a year earlier, which aligns with a growth minded thesis around the business size getting larger on this time frame.
    • However, that same trailing 12 month period still produced a US$6.5 million net loss and negative EPS of US$0.05, which keeps the bullish story tied to growth rather than to ongoing profitability.

Curious how these numbers stack up against what other investors are saying about the stock right now? 📊 Read the what the Community is saying about Gold Resource.

P/S at 3.1x with ongoing losses and high volatility

  • The stock trades on a P/S of 3.1x compared with 2.5x for the US Metals & Mining industry and 3.0x for peers. At the same time, the company remained unprofitable on a trailing 12 month view and has less than one year of cash runway alongside recent shareholder dilution.
  • Critics highlight valuation and balance sheet risk, and the current data gives them several concrete reference points to work with:
    • The combination of a P/S above both industry and peer averages, plus a trailing 12 month net loss of US$6.5 million, means investors are paying more per dollar of revenue for a business that has yet to report positive earnings over that period.
    • High share price volatility over the past three months and substantial dilution in the last year tie the risk case directly to how the company is funding itself and how unpredictable shorter term returns may be around the current US$1.12 share price.

Next Steps

Don't just look at this quarter; the real story is in the long-term trend. We've done an in-depth analysis on Gold Resource's growth and its valuation to see if today's price is a bargain. Add the company to your watchlist or portfolio now so you don't miss the next big move.

With the mix of risk and optimism in these numbers, this is a moment to look closely at the details yourself and move quickly to form an informed view by checking the 1 key reward and 4 important warning signs.

See What Else Is Out There

Between the trailing 12 month net loss, inconsistent quarterly profitability, short cash runway, and shareholder dilution, Gold Resource still carries meaningful financial and balance sheet risk.

data.title: Constellation Energy Reshapes Portfolio With LS Power Sale For Calpine Deal
data.description:
• Constellation Energy (NasdaqGS:CEG) has agreed to sell a major portfolio of natural gas fired power plants to LS Power.
  • The planned divestiture is designed to satisfy antitrust conditions tied to Constellation's acquisition of Calpine.
  • This transaction represents the largest portion of regulatory required asset sales linked to the Calpine deal and is subject to approvals from the U.S. Department of Justice and FERC.

Constellation Energy operates a large U.S. power generation and energy supply business, and the Calpine acquisition is a significant step in reshaping its position in the sector. By selling a sizeable group of gas fired assets to LS Power, the company is aligning with antitrust and policy requirements while adjusting its mix of generation assets.

For you as an investor, this move is important because it affects the structure of Constellation's portfolio and how it competes in the U.S. power market. The outcome of regulatory reviews and the final scope of asset sales will influence the company's capital allocation choices and exposure to different power technologies.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Constellation Energy by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Constellation Energy.

We've flagged 3 risks for Constellation Energy. See which could impact your investment.

This asset sale sits at the intersection of Constellation Energy’s push into AI-linked nuclear power and regulators’ focus on competition in wholesale electricity markets. By agreeing to sell about 4.4 gigawatts of gas-fired capacity in the PJM region for roughly US$5b, Constellation is effectively trading some flexible thermal generation for the ability to complete the Calpine acquisition and lock in a much larger platform. That matters if you are following the company’s efforts to supply long-duration, carbon-free power to data centers run by Microsoft, Meta and other hyperscalers. A tighter focus on nuclear and geothermal could make the business more aligned with those contracts, while a smaller gas footprint may leave Constellation comparatively less exposed to merchant price swings than peers such as NextEra Energy, Duke Energy or Vistra. On the other hand, giving up dispatchable gas capacity could leave the portfolio more reliant on the nuclear fleet for reliability and on Calpine’s remaining assets for flexibility, which is worth keeping in mind as electricity demand tied to AI infrastructure grows.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Constellation Energy Narrative

  • The divestiture helps clear a key regulatory hurdle for the Calpine acquisition, supporting the narrative that Constellation can expand capacity and product offerings to serve long-term, data center focused contracts.
  • Shifting a portion of gas-fired assets out of the portfolio could challenge the idea that Constellation will rely on a broad mix of nuclear and gas to manage rising AI data center demand if future reliability depends more heavily on the remaining fleet.
  • The specific loss of 4.4 gigawatts of PJM gas capacity and the US$5b transaction value are not directly reflected in the narrative, which may mean investors using that story should reassess assumptions about future fuel mix and cash flow resilience.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Constellation Energy to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Concentration risk could rise if Constellation leans even more on its nuclear fleet and a smaller set of gas plants to meet high, data center driven demand.
  • ⚠️ The transaction still depends on approvals from the U.S. Department of Justice and FERC, so delays, additional conditions or a need for further asset sales would add execution risk.
  • 🎁 Completing this sale is a key step toward fully closing out antitrust commitments, which can reduce regulatory overhang from the Calpine deal.
  • 🎁 Recycling US$5b from gas assets may give Constellation more flexibility to fund nuclear uprates, restarts and long-term infrastructure serving AI-related customers.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, focus on three things. First, whether the DOJ and FERC sign off on this transaction on the expected timetable and whether any extra remedies are requested. Second, how Constellation redeploys the US$5b in proceeds, especially across nuclear uprates, potential share repurchases or further energy infrastructure projects. Third, how the combined Constellation and Calpine portfolio looks once all required sales are complete, including the balance between nuclear, gas and geothermal capacity and how that compares with peers that are also chasing AI-related electricity demand. Those pieces will shape how durable the company’s cash flows look over time.

data.title: SoundHound AI CFO Change Puts Focus On Growth And Cash Discipline
data.description:
• SoundHound AI (NasdaqGM:SOUN) announced a CFO transition, with Nitesh Sharan leaving to join a quantum computing company.
  • Co founder and board member James Hom, currently Chief Product Officer, has been appointed interim CFO while a search for a permanent replacement is underway.

For investors watching SoundHound AI at a current share price of $7.39, this executive change comes after a mixed stretch for the stock. The share price is down 5.1% over the past week, 0.9% over the past month, and 30.3% year to date, while the three year return is described as very large. That profile can make leadership stability and clear financial oversight feel especially important.

The interim CFO role going to a co founder and existing board member may help preserve continuity while the company looks for a permanent hire. As SoundHound AI continues to pursue rapid expansion and high profile partnerships, investors are likely to watch how this transition affects capital allocation, cost discipline, and the pace of execution.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for SoundHound AI by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on SoundHound AI.

Does the team leading SoundHound AI have what it takes? See our full breakdown of the management team's track record and compensation.

For investors, the CFO transition lands at a busy time for SoundHound AI, with new product launches, enterprise partnerships, and customer deployments all moving at once. Losing a sitting CFO can raise questions about funding plans, cost control, and reporting quality, especially for a company that is still loss making and investing heavily in growth. In this case, management has framed Nitesh Sharan’s departure as personal and unrelated to accounting or policy disagreements, which helps reduce the risk of this being read as a red flag on financial reporting. Bringing co founder and long time executive James Hom into the interim CFO seat, after previously serving as CFO, adds institutional memory and product insight, but also concentrates responsibilities across finance, product, and the board. For you, the key question is whether SoundHound can run large enterprise deals and product rollouts while keeping a tight handle on cash, margins, and capital needs during this handover.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The SoundHound AI Narrative

  • The move to an interim CFO who helped build SoundHound from its early days aligns with the narrative of pairing rapid expansion in autos and enterprise with leadership that knows the product and long-term strategy well.
  • The transition also highlights a narrative risk that heavy investment and ongoing losses require strong financial stewardship, and any disruption in the finance seat could complicate execution on complex agentic AI projects and partnerships.
  • The most followed narratives focus heavily on growth assumptions and partnerships but may not fully reflect succession planning or how a changing finance team could influence funding choices and cost discipline.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for SoundHound AI to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged that SoundHound AI is currently unprofitable and not forecast to reach profitability in the next few years, so any slowdown in financial execution during a CFO handover could make losses harder to control.
  • ⚠️ A change in the finance leader while the company is signing large, sometimes lumpy enterprise deals across autos, restaurants, and enterprise AI can add uncertainty around how consistently revenue, cash burn, and capital needs are managed.
  • 🎁 Having a co founder and former CFO step in as interim CFO brings deep familiarity with SoundHound’s technology, customer base, and deal structures, which can help keep decision making consistent while a permanent hire is identified.
  • 🎁 The company’s recent partnerships in areas like healthcare and retail, plus traction with agentic AI in vehicles alongside large players such as Alphabet, Amazon, and Microsoft, give the incoming permanent CFO a growing commercial base to work with.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, focus on how smoothly SoundHound AI manages this handover: look for timely financial reporting, clear commentary on cash runway and funding options, and any updates on hiring a permanent CFO. It is also worth tracking whether the company maintains momentum in new deployments with partners in automotive, healthcare, and retail while keeping operating losses and capital allocation aligned with its long-term ambitions.

data.title: Rocatinlimab Halt Puts Spotlight On Amgen Pipeline Risks And Valuation
data.description:
• Amgen and Kyowa Kirin have discontinued all clinical trials of rocatinlimab after newly identified safety risks, including malignancy cases.
  • The decision removes a high profile immunology asset from Amgen's pipeline and halts further development of the drug.
  • This move follows internal safety review work and affects ongoing studies across multiple indications.

For investors watching NasdaqGS:AMGN, the rocatinlimab decision comes at a time when the stock is trading at $349.92, with a 1 year return of 14.6% and a 3 year return of 64.9%. The 5 year return of 66.2% places recent volatility, including a 4.9% decline over the past week and a 6.3% decline over the past month, in the context of a longer track record of shareholder returns.

Rocatimlimab's removal from the pipeline may influence how you think about Amgen's research focus in immunology and inflammation, as well as the risk profile around late stage assets. The key questions now center on how management reallocates R&D spending, which programs step forward to fill this gap, and how safety considerations may shape future portfolio choices for NasdaqGS:AMGN.

data.title: Novartis Adds Rare Disease Depth And Cardiovascular Optionality For Investors
data.description:
• Novartis received FDA approval for Cosentyx to treat pediatric hidradenitis suppurativa, expanding options for adolescent patients with this chronic skin disease.
  • The approval makes Cosentyx the first IL-17A inhibitor cleared for this pediatric indication in the United States.
  • Novartis also signed a licensing agreement with Unnatural Products to develop macrocyclic peptide therapeutics for cardiovascular diseases.
  • The deal targets previously hard-to-address cardiovascular drug targets and includes potential milestone opportunities.

Approach 1: Axcelis Technologies Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to today using a required rate of return. It is essentially asking what Axcelis Technologies' future cash generation is worth in today's dollars.

For Axcelis, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $105.9 million, with analysts providing a future free cash flow estimate of $231 million for 2023. Beyond the usual analyst horizon, Simply Wall St extrapolates cash flows out to 2035, with annual projections such as $96.8 million in 2026 and $100.1 million in 2035, each discounted back to reflect time and risk.

Aggregating these discounted projections gives an estimated intrinsic value of about $35.03 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$85.74, the DCF output suggests the stock is around 144.8% above this model's fair value estimate. This indicates a comparatively rich valuation based on these assumptions.

Approach 2: Axcelis Technologies Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to see how much you are paying for each dollar of earnings. It reflects what the market is willing to pay today based on what it expects from future earnings and how risky those earnings appear to be.

Higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk usually support a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk tend to justify a lower P/E.
